# Terraform Provider for Coolify

The Coolify provider enables Terraform to manage [Coolify](https://coolify.io/) v4 resources including servers, applications, databases, services, projects, and environments.

This provider interacts with the Coolify API to provide Infrastructure as Code (IaC) capabilities for your self-hosted Platform as a Service.

## Version Compatibility

- **Coolify**: v4.0.0-beta.381 and later
- **Terraform**: 0.13+
- **Go**: 1.24+ (if building from source)

~> **Note:** This provider requires Coolify v4 (beta) and is not compatible with Coolify v3.

## Prerequisites

Before using this provider, you'll need:

1. A running Coolify v4 instance
2. An API token with appropriate permissions (generate in Coolify dashboard under API settings)
3. Network access from your Terraform execution environment to your Coolify instance

## Authentication

The provider supports two authentication methods:

### Environment Variable (Recommended)

```bash
export COOLIFY_TOKEN="your-api-token-here"
export COOLIFY_ENDPOINT="https://coolify.example.com/api/v1"
```

### Provider Configuration

```terraform
provider "coolify" {
  token    = "your-api-token-here"  # Not recommended - use environment variable instead
  endpoint = "https://coolify.example.com/api/v1"
}
```

For detailed setup instructions, see [SETUP.md](https://github.com/patrikwm/terraform-provider-coolify/blob/main/SETUP.md).

## Example Usage

### Basic Provider Configuration

```terraform
terraform {
  required_providers {
    coolify = {
      source  = "patrikwm/coolify"
      version = "~> 0.1"
    }
  }
  required_version = ">= 1.0"
}

provider "coolify" {
  # Authentication via COOLIFY_TOKEN environment variable
  # endpoint defaults to https://app.coolify.io/api/v1
  
  # Optional: Configure retry behavior
  retry {
    attempts = 5
    min_wait = 1
    max_wait = 30
  }
}
```

### Complete Infrastructure Example

This example demonstrates provisioning a complete Coolify-managed infrastructure:

```terraform
# Generate SSH key for server access
resource "tls_private_key" "server_key" {
  algorithm = "ED25519"
}

# Register the private key with Coolify
resource "coolify_private_key" "main" {
  name        = "Production Server Key"
  description = "Managed by Terraform"
  private_key = tls_private_key.server_key.private_key_pem
}

# Add a server to Coolify
resource "coolify_server" "production" {
  name             = "production-server-01"
  description      = "Production application server"
  ip               = "192.168.1.100"
  port             = 22
  user             = "root"
  private_key_uuid = coolify_private_key.main.uuid
  instant_validate = true
}

# Create a project for organization
resource "coolify_project" "web_app" {
  name        = "Web Application"
  description = "Production web application stack"
}

# Create an environment within the project
resource "coolify_environment" "production" {
  project_uuid = coolify_project.web_app.uuid
  name         = "production"
}

# Deploy a PostgreSQL database
resource "coolify_postgresql_database" "app_db" {
  server_uuid         = coolify_server.production.uuid
  name                = "app-database"
  description         = "Application database"
  postgres_user       = "app_user"
  postgres_password   = "secure-password-here"  # Use variables in production
  postgres_db         = "app_production"
  postgres_conf       = "max_connections=200"
  instant_deploy      = true
}

# Deploy a service using docker-compose
resource "coolify_service" "redis_cache" {
  server_uuid = coolify_server.production.uuid
  name        = "redis-cache"
  description = "Redis cache for application"
  
  docker_compose = yamlencode({
    services = {
      redis = {
        image = "redis:7-alpine"
        ports = ["6379:6379"]
        volumes = ["redis-data:/data"]
      }
    }
    volumes = {
      redis-data = {}
    }
  })
  
  instant_deploy = true
}
```

### Using Data Sources

Query existing Coolify resources:

```terraform
# Find all servers
data "coolify_servers" "all" {}

# Find a specific server by UUID
data "coolify_server" "production" {
  uuid = "server-uuid-here"
}

# List all projects
data "coolify_projects" "all" {}

# Get team information
data "coolify_team" "current" {
  uuid = "team-uuid-here"
}

# Use data sources in resources
resource "coolify_service" "app" {
  server_uuid = data.coolify_server.production.uuid
  # ... rest of configuration
}
```

## Supported Resources

| Type | Resource | Data Source |
|------|----------|-------------|
| Infrastructure | `coolify_server`, `coolify_private_key` | `coolify_server(s)`, `coolify_private_key(s)` |
| Organization | `coolify_project`, `coolify_environment` | `coolify_project(s)`, `coolify_team(s)` |
| Databases | `coolify_postgresql_database`, `coolify_mysql_database` | - |
| Services | `coolify_service`, `coolify_service_envs` | `coolify_service` |
| Applications | `coolify_application_envs` | `coolify_application(s)` |
| Metadata | - | `coolify_server_domains`, `coolify_server_resources` |

For detailed documentation on each resource and data source, see the navigation menu.

## Import Existing Resources

Most resources support Terraform import:

```bash
# Import a server
terraform import coolify_server.production server-uuid-here

# Import a project
terraform import coolify_project.web_app project-uuid-here

# Import an environment (format: project_uuid/environment_name)
terraform import coolify_environment.prod project-uuid/production

# Import a service
terraform import coolify_service.redis service-uuid-here
```

Refer to individual resource documentation for resource-specific import formats.

## Troubleshooting

### Connection Issues

If you're experiencing connection problems:

1. Verify your `COOLIFY_ENDPOINT` is correct (should end with `/api/v1`)
2. Check your API token has necessary permissions in Coolify dashboard
3. Ensure network connectivity: `curl -H "Authorization: Bearer $COOLIFY_TOKEN" $COOLIFY_ENDPOINT/teams`
4. Enable debug logging: `TF_LOG=DEBUG terraform apply`

### Common Errors

**"401 Unauthorized"**
- Check your `COOLIFY_TOKEN` is set correctly
- Verify token hasn't expired in Coolify dashboard
- Ensure token has appropriate permissions

**"Resource not found"**
- Verify the resource UUID exists in Coolify
- Check you're connected to the correct Coolify instance
- Some resources may have been deleted outside Terraform

**"Timeout" errors**
- Increase retry settings in provider configuration
- Check Coolify instance is responsive
- Verify no firewall blocking the connection

### Debug Mode

Enable detailed logging for troubleshooting:

```bash
export TF_LOG=DEBUG
export TF_LOG_PATH=./terraform.log
terraform apply
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `token` (String, Sensitive) Coolify token. If not set, checks env for `COOLIFY_TOKEN`.

### Optional

- `endpoint` (String) Coolify endpoint. If not set, checks env for `COOLIFY_ENDPOINT`. Default: `https://app.coolify.io/api/v1`.
- `retry` (Attributes) Configuration for the HTTP retry behavior (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--retry))

<a id="nestedatt--retry"></a>
### Nested Schema for `retry`

Optional:

- `attempts` (Number) Maximum number of retries for HTTP requests. Default: 4
- `max_wait` (Number) Maximum time to wait between retries in seconds. Default: 30
- `min_wait` (Number) Minimum time to wait between retries in seconds. Default: 1
